authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 453 - Funeral Industry Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes the controlling federal Funeral Rule text for funeral-provider disclosures, itemized statement, embalming representations, casket and outer burial container handling, and arrangement recordkeeping. These controls determine when an arrangement conference can progress or close.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: price-list disclosure, itemized selection statement, embalming-disclosure control, and completion-evidence review
used_for: completion refusal when disclosure evidence is missing

authoritative_sources.title: Complying with the Funeral Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal Funeral Rule regulator. This guidance is directly written for funeral providers and translates the rule into practical arrangement-conference workflows, price-list timing, telephone price handling, selection statement handling, and disclosure terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: arrangement intake, price discussion, goods and services selection, and disclosure exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: Funeral Directors' Handbook on Death Registration and Fetal Death Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, National Center for Health Statistics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC/NCHS publishes the national vital-statistics manual for funeral directors. It defines death-registration duties, demographic collection, medical-certifier coordination, and filing dependencies that arrangement conferences must treat as evidence gates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: death-certificate demographic intake, medical-certifier dependency tracking, and vital-record filing readiness
used_for: blocked workflow handling when vital-record data is missing

authoritative_sources.title: Consumer Guide to Funeral and Cemetery Purchases
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs Cemetery and Funeral Bureau
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's funeral and cemetery regulator publishes practical public guidance on right to control disposition, GPL/CPL and itemized statement expectations, embalming authorization, burial, cremation, hydrolysis, cemetery, licensing, and consumer complaint pathways. It is authoritative for California and a useful example of state-level jurisdictional variance that this lane must surface rather than universalize.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: family authority validation, disposition path selection, cemetery or crematory dependency handling, and state-specific escalation
used_for: burial, cremation, hydrolysis, and cemetery terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Committal Service
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, National Cemetery Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: VA National Cemetery Administration is the official source for national cemetery committal service constraints and burial-document expectations. It constrains arrangement-conference handling when a family requests veterans cemetery committal scheduling or eligibility confirmation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: veterans cemetery committal dependency review and adjacent-owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: Application of provisions of the Bloodborne Pathogens standard to Funeral and Nursing Homes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's official interpretation explains funeral-home obligations around human remains, occupational exposure, and protective-equipment considerations. It is relevant to arrangement-conference refusal triggers when a requester asks this lane to authorize body handling, preparation, or safety-critical actions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: embalming, preparation, remains-handling, and safety-boundary escalation

workflow_stages.name: Conference intake and family-authority validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize first-call, decedent, location-of-remains, removal, authorized arranger, person authorized to direct disposition, preneed instructions, PADD, power-of-attorney, and jurisdiction context before proceeding.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Price-list disclosure and selection package control
workflow_stages.description: Track GPL, Casket Price List, outer burial container price list, package selections, cash-advance items, estimates, and statement of funeral goods and services selected as required arrangement-conference evidence.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Disposition-path planning and destination dependency mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map burial, direct cremation, immediate burial, cremation, hydrolysis, green burial, cemetery, crematory, committal, interment-right, container, authorization, and scheduling dependencies without overruling destination requirements.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Vital-record, permit, and service dependency tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track death-certificate demographic data, medical certifier or coroner status, disposition permit, obituary, clergy, music, flowers, transportation, and external service dependencies as lifecycle blockers or next-owner tasks.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, rework, and adjacent-owner escalation
workflow_stages.description: Hold or reroute cases with disputed family authority, missing vital records, unsigned embalming authorization, cemetery or crematory conflicts, VA burial dependencies, policy/source conflicts, payment exceptions, or grief-support requests.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Close only when authorized-arranger evidence, price-list disclosure state, selected goods and services, signed statement, authorization status, vital-record references, disposition permit posture, and destination confirmations are explicit.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: decedent case record, first-call notes, removal or transfer record, custody status, and location-of-remains note
authorized arranger or person authorized to direct disposition evidence, preneed contract, written instructions, advance directive, PADD, or power-of-attorney references
General Price List, Casket Price List, outer burial container price list, selected goods and services, cash-advance items, package estimate, and statement of funeral goods and services selected
embalming authorization or declination, viewing or service request, refrigeration or delay note, and licensed funeral director or embalmer owner
death-certificate demographic intake, medical certifier status, coroner or medical examiner hold, burial transit or disposition permit, and eDRS filing reference

cemetery interment-right record, crematory or hydrolysis authorization, burial container or alternative container requirement, committal schedule, VA burial document reference, obituary, clergy, flowers, music, transportation, and payment references
exception notes, rework checklists, escalation notes, completion evidence records, and audit trail entries
systems_of_record: family-service CRM or preneed management system
electronic death-registration system or vital-record portal
cemetery management, interment-right, and committal scheduling system
payment, POS, invoicing, trust, insurance, or preneed funding ledger
medical examiner, coroner, hospital release, or certifier communication record
VA National Cemetery Administration scheduling or benefits reference
task, exception, compliance, and audit workflow system

allowed: Classify arrangement-conference requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, family-authority context, source context, and systems-of-record references required to proceed.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete lifecycle state based on documented disclosure, selection, authorization, vital-record, destination, and next-owner evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, family-service workflow summaries, exception notes, completion records, and routing packages.
escalate: Authorized arranger, right-to-control disposition, preneed instruction, PADD, power-of-attorney, jurisdiction, or family-consensus facts are missing or disputed.
Death-certificate, medical certifier, coroner, disposition permit, cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, or VA burial documents are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires legal interpretation, licensed funeral directing or embalming judgment, cemetery administration signoff, crematory authorization, benefits eligibility, payment approval, grief counseling, safety-critical action, or policy override.
Public sources, jurisdiction rules, cemetery or crematory requirements, tenant policy, or preneed documents materially conflict.
refuse: Mark the arrangement conference complete without GPL/CPL/OBCPL state, selected goods and services, signed statement or equivalent evidence, authorization posture, vital-record references, and destination confirmation.
Authorize embalming, body preparation, cremation, hydrolysis, burial, transportation, or final disposition outside licensed or authorized owners.
Resolve legal family-authority disputes, approve financing or payment exceptions, adjudicate VA or insurance benefits, or provide grief, legal, clinical, or estate advice.

domain_failure_modes: the lane treats the funeral industry label as cosmetic and produces a generic case update without GPL, selected-services, disposition, death-registration, or destination evidence
an arrangement is marked complete without an itemized statement, selected goods and services, required price-list evidence, or cash-advance estimates
family-authority or right-to-control disposition conflict is treated as an ordinary preference dispute instead of legal or compliance escalation
death certificate, medical certifier, coroner, or disposition permit dependencies are skipped and downstream burial or cremation scheduling fails
embalming, viewing, preparation, cremation, hydrolysis, or body-handling requests are authorized without licensed-owner, authorization, or safety evidence
cemetery, crematory, VA, or hydrolysis requirements are overwritten by conference convenience instead of routed to the correct owner
tenant package-pricing, pre-need, or payment rules are applied despite conflict with public source requirements or missing jurisdiction facts
grief support, estate advice, payment approval, or benefits eligibility is absorbed by the lane because it occurred during an arrangement conference

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Right-to-control disposition hierarchy, embalming authorization forms, cremation or hydrolysis authorization, disposition permits, cemetery regulation, and preneed-funding rules vary materially by jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The validated pack treats jurisdiction, tenant policy, facility requirements, and licensed-owner authority as mandatory runtime facts. It does not guess outcomes where state or facility rules differ.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant systems may use different names for arrangement case records, eDRS references, statement artifacts, and cemetery or crematory confirmations.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant-specific system names and record locations before execution. The pack defines the required source-of-truth classes and refusal behavior when mappings are missing.

notes: The research output constrains the final pack to funeral, cemetery, crematory, vital-record, and end-of-life arrangement evidence rather than generic workflow execution.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because all jurisdiction and tenant-specific variance is converted into explicit prerequisite, escalation, or refusal handling.
